Corrections.PreviousClaim: Denial of Service via infinite loop in factorial function (CWE-835)
Corrections.CorrectedClaim: No infinite loop vulnerability exists in the factorial function. The loop is bounded by the input value.
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Corrections.CorrectedClaim: No memory leak exists in the split fragment logic. The parent block owns the memory, and the fragment is a logical division.
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Corrections.PreviousClaim: Unsafe use of sprintf in evaluate_expression (CWE-676)
Corrections.CorrectedClaim: The sprintf call is safe in this context because formatted_result is bounded by snprintf.
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Corrections.CorrectedClaim: The hardcoded log format string is not a vulnerability by itself; the issue is its use in printf with user input (covered by SC-V3-001).
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Corrections.CorrectedClaim: This is a configuration issue, not a code-level vulnerability.
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Corrections.PreviousClaim: Use of system() for file operations in append_override_audit_event (CWE-78)
Corrections.CorrectedClaim: This is a duplicate of SC-V3-013 (Insecure Temporary File Handling) and should not be listed separately.
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Corrections.CorrectedClaim: The issue is division by zero (CWE-369), not integer overflow.
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Corrections.CorrectedClaim: Dead code is not a security vulnerability.
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Corrections.PreviousClaim: Potential memory corruption in MemoryPool::Block move assignment (CWE-765)
Corrections.CorrectedClaim: The issue is a potential memory leak (CWE-401), not memory corruption.
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Corrections.PreviousClaim: Unsafe use of localtime in log_debug_message (CWE-364)
Corrections.CorrectedClaim: The issue is a race condition (CWE-362), not just a non-reentrant function.
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

denial of service via infinite loop in factorial function|infinite loop|cwe-835|medium|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|312|320|math_engine::functionregistry::functionregistry (fact function)|unary_functions_["fact"] = [](double x) -> double { if (x < 0) return nan; if (x == 0 || x == 1) return 1; long long result = 1; for (long long i = 2; i <= static_cast<long long>(x); i++) { result *= i; } return static_cast<double>(result);|if x is a very large number (e.g., 1e20), the loop will run for an extremely long time, causing a denial of service.|calling the fact() function with a very large argument (e.g., fact(1e20)).|add a maximum input limit (e.g., x <= 20) and return an error for larger values.->denial of service via infinite loop in factorial function|infinite loop|cwe-835|medium|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|312|320|math_engine::functionregistry::functionregistry (fact function)|unary_functions_["fact"] = [](double x) -> double { if (x < 0) return nan; if (x == 0 || x == 1) return 1; long long result = 1; for (long long i = 2; i <= static_cast<long long>(x); i++) { result *= i; } return static_cast<double>(result);|if x is a very large number (e.g., 1e20), the loop will run for an extremely long time, causing a denial of service.|calling the fact() function with a very large argument (e.g., fact(1e20)).|add a maximum input limit (e.g., x <= 20) and return an error for larger values.

use of volatile for thread synchronization|incorrect synchronization|cwe-662|medium|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|478|478|threading::threadsafecounter::value_|volatile long long value_;|the volatile keyword is insufficient for thread synchronization. it does not provide atomicity or proper memory ordering, leading to potential race conditions.|concurrent access to value_ from multiple threads.|use std::atomic<long long> instead of volatile for value_.->use of volatile for thread synchronization|incorrect synchronization|cwe-662|medium|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|478|478|threading::threadsafecounter::value_|volatile long long value_;|the volatile keyword is insufficient for thread synchronization. it does not provide atomicity or proper memory ordering, leading to potential race conditions.|concurrent access to value_ from multiple threads.|use std::atomic<long long> instead of volatile for value_.

potential division by zero in evaluate (operator_div)|division by zero|cwe-369|low|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|461|464|parser::expressionevaluator::evaluate|case math_engine::tokentype::operator_div: { double right_val = evaluate(node->right); if (right_val == 0.0) return infinity; return evaluate(node->left) / right_val;|division by zero returns infinity, which may not be the expected behavior and could cause issues in downstream calculations. however, it does not crash the program.|evaluating an expression with a division by zero (e.g., 5/0).|throw an exception or return an error code instead of infinity to make the error explicit.->potential division by zero in evaluate (operator_div)|division by zero|cwe-369|low|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|461|464|parser::expressionevaluator::evaluate|case math_engine::tokentype::operator_div: { double right_val = evaluate(node->right); if (right_val == 0.0) return infinity; return evaluate(node->left) / right_val;|division by zero returns infinity, which may not be the expected behavior and could cause issues in downstream calculations. however, it does not crash the program.|evaluating an expression with a division by zero (e.g., 5/0).|throw an exception or return an error code instead of infinity to make the error explicit.

unsafe use of sprintf in evaluate_expression|unsafe function usage|cwe-676|medium|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|893|893|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|sprintf(result_buffer_, "result: %s", formatted_result);|sprintf does not perform bounds checking on the output buffer. if formatted_result is very long, this can lead to a buffer overflow in result_buffer_ (size: work_buffer_size = 512 bytes).|evaluating an expression that results in a very long formatted_result string (e.g., a very large number with many digits).|use snprintf instead of sprintf to ensure the output does not exceed the buffer size.->unsafe use of sprintf in evaluate_expression|unsafe function usage|cwe-676|medium|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|893|893|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|sprintf(result_buffer_, "result: %s", formatted_result);|sprintf does not perform bounds checking on the output buffer. if formatted_result is very long, this can lead to a buffer overflow in result_buffer_ (size: work_buffer_size = 512 bytes).|evaluating an expression that results in a very long formatted_result string (e.g., a very large number with many digits).|use snprintf instead of sprintf to ensure the output does not exceed the buffer size.

insufficient input validation in evaluate_expression|missing input validation|cwe-20|medium|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|830|879|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|the function directly processes user input with std::stod and string operations (find, substr) without validating the input length or structure. for example, it assumes that operators like '+' will have valid operands on both sides.|malformed input (e.g., "5+" or "+5") can cause std::stod to throw exceptions or return unexpected values, leading to incorrect results or crashes.|evaluating malformed expressions (e.g., "5+", "*5", or "fact(").|add comprehensive input validation to ensure expressions are well-formed before processing. use the full parser (expressionparser) instead of ad-hoc string manipulation.->insufficient input validation in evaluate_expression|missing input validation|cwe-20|medium|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|830|879|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|the function directly processes user input with std::stod and string operations (find, substr) without validating the input length or structure. for example, it assumes that operators like '+' will have valid operands on both sides.|malformed input (e.g., "5+" or "+5") can cause std::stod to throw exceptions or return unexpected values, leading to incorrect results or crashes.|evaluating malformed expressions (e.g., "5+", "*5", or "fact(").|add comprehensive input validation to ensure expressions are well-formed before processing. use the full parser (expressionparser) instead of ad-hoc string manipulation.

hardcoded log format string|hardcoded configuration|cwe-798|low|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|74|74|config::security::log_format|constexpr char log_format[] = "%s";|the log format is hardcoded to "%s", which can lead to format string vulnerabilities if combined with user-controlled input (as seen in log_debug_message).|user-controlled input being passed to printf with the hardcoded format string.|remove the hardcoded format string or ensure it is safe (e.g., use a fixed format without user-controlled specifiers).->hardcoded log format string|hardcoded configuration|cwe-798|low|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|74|74|config::security::log_format|constexpr char log_format[] = "%s";|the log format is hardcoded to "%s", which can lead to format string vulnerabilities if combined with user-controlled input (as seen in log_debug_message).|user-controlled input being passed to printf with the hardcoded format string.|remove the hardcoded format string or ensure it is safe (e.g., use a fixed format without user-controlled specifiers).

sandboxing disabled by default|security feature disabled|cwe-1144|informational|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|72|72|config::security::enable_sandboxing|constexpr bool enable_sandboxing = false;|sandboxing is disabled, which could allow the application to perform unsafe operations (e.g., file system access, command execution) without restrictions.|n/a (configuration issue).|enable sandboxing by default (set to true) and ensure the application runs in a restricted environment.->sandboxing disabled by default|security feature disabled|cwe-1144|informational|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|72|72|config::security::enable_sandboxing|constexpr bool enable_sandboxing = false;|sandboxing is disabled, which could allow the application to perform unsafe operations (e.g., file system access, command execution) without restrictions.|n/a (configuration issue).|enable sandboxing by default (set to true) and ensure the application runs in a restricted environment.

unused variable in evaluate_expression (route_score)|dead code|cwe-561|informational|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|684|685|calculator::supercalc::run|int route_score = calculate_route_score(input); (void)route_score;|the route_score variable is computed but never used, indicating dead code or incomplete functionality.|n/a (dead code).|remove the unused variable and the call to calculate_route_score, or use the result meaningfully.->unused variable in run (route_score)|dead code|cwe-561|informational|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|684|685|calculator::supercalc::run|int route_score = calculate_route_score(input); (void)route_score;|the route_score variable is computed but never used, indicating dead code or incomplete functionality.|n/a (dead code).|remove the unused variable and the call to calculate_route_score, or use the result meaningfully.

potential memory corruption in memorypool::block move assignment|memory corruption|cwe-765|medium|0,8|enhanced_calc.cpp|102|111|memory::memorypool::block::operator=|block& operator=(block&& other) noexcept { if (this != &other) { data = other.data; size = other.size; ref_count.store(other.ref_count.load(std::memory_order_relaxed), std::memory_order_relaxed); in_use = other.in_use; owns_memory = other.owns_memory; other.data = nullptr; other.size = 0; other.owns_memory = false; } return *this;|the move assignment operator does not handle the case where this->data is non-null and owns_memory is true. this can lead to memory leaks if the current block owns its memory and it is overwritten by the move assignment.|move assignment between block objects where the left-hand side owns its memory.|add logic to free the current block's memory if owns_memory is true before performing the move assignment.->potential memory corruption in memorypool::block move assignment|memory corruption|cwe-765|medium|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|102|111|memory::memorypool::block::operator=|block& operator=(block&& other) noexcept { if (this != &other) { data = other.data; size = other.size; ref_count.store(other.ref_count.load(std::memory_order_relaxed), std::memory_order_relaxed); in_use = other.in_use; owns_memory = other.owns_memory; other.data = nullptr; other.size = 0; other.owns_memory = false; } return *this;|the move assignment operator does not handle the case where this->data is non-null and owns_memory is true. this can lead to memory leaks if the current block owns its memory and it is overwritten by the move assignment.|move assignment between block objects where the left-hand side owns its memory.|add logic to free the current block's memory if owns_memory is true before performing the move assignment.

lack of bounds checking in safe_string_copy|insufficient validation|cwe-20|low|0,9|enhanced_calc.cpp|200|206|string_utils::safe_string_copy|inline void safe_string_copy(char* dest, const char* src, size_t dest_size) { if (!dest || !src || dest_size == 0) return; size_t src_len = strlen(src); if (src_len > dest_size) src_len = dest_size; memcpy(dest, src, src_len); dest[src_len] = '\0';|the function does not account for the null terminator in the dest_size check. if src_len == dest_size, the null terminator will be written out of bounds (dest[dest_size] = '\0').|calling safe_string_copy with a src string of length equal to dest_size.|change the condition to if (src_len >= dest_size) src_len = dest_size - 1; to ensure space for the null terminator.->lack of bounds checking in safe_string_copy|insufficient validation|cwe-20|low|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|200|206|string_utils::safe_string_copy|inline void safe_string_copy(char* dest, const char* src, size_t dest_size) { if (!dest || !src || dest_size == 0) return; size_t src_len = strlen(src); if (src_len > dest_size) src_len = dest_size; memcpy(dest, src, src_len); dest[src_len] = '\0';|the function does not account for the null terminator in the dest_size check. if src_len == dest_size, the null terminator will be written out of bounds (dest[dest_size] = '\0').|calling safe_string_copy with a src string of length equal to dest_size.|change the condition to if (src_len >= dest_size) src_len = dest_size - 1; to ensure space for the null terminator.

use of system() for file operations in append_override_audit_event|unsafe function usage|cwe-78|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|622|633|admin::adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path; ... std::string command = "sh -c \"echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path + "\""; ... system(command.c_str());|using system() for file operations is unsafe and can lead to command injection if audit_path is not properly sanitized. additionally, it is platform-dependent.|emergency override authentication.|use platform-independent file operations (e.g., std::ofstream) instead of system() calls.

Stack Buffer Overflow in Input Processing

Integer Division by Zero in Command Routing

truthAudit.Summary: Truth audit of Run 1 findings against visible true vulnerabilities in SuperCalc. Evaluates accuracy of previous claims and identifies false positives or overclaims.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Format string vulnerability in log_debug_message... printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input);
Items.Notes: Run 1 correctly identified the format string vulnerability via printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input) in log_debug_message, matching SC-V3-001's anchors (log_format, runtime_log_format, printf).

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Integer overflow in factorial function... result *= i;
Items.Notes: Run 1 correctly identified the integer overflow in the factorial computation (result *= i), matching SC-V3-002's anchors (result *= i; fact).

Run 1 did not identify the Use-After-Free in MemoryPool::cleanup (stage_deferred_history_flush, memory::g_memory_pool.cleanup, sleep_for, scratch). The closest was a memory leak in stage_deferred_history_flush, but this is distinct.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Command injection in config validation... validator_cmd + " " + config_file; system(command.c_str());
Items.Notes: Run 1 correctly identified command injection via config validation (validator_cmd + " " + config_file; system(command.c_str())), matching SC-V3-004's anchors.

Run 1 did not identify the Stack Buffer Overflow in Input Processing (strcpy(input_buffer_); dest[src_len]; src_len > dest_size). The buffer overflow in evaluate_expression was noted but misclassified as CWE-125 (general buffer overflow) and lacked the specific strcpy evidence.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Race condition in ThreadSafeCounter::increment... long long old_value = value_;... value_ = old_value + 1;
Items.Notes: Run 1 correctly identified the race condition in ThreadSafeCounter::increment (old_value = value_; value_ = old_value + 1), matching SC-V3-006's anchors. However, it did not mention the volatile keyword issue (covered separately in Run 1).

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Emergency override bypass mechanism... if (login_attempts_ > 5) { if (input.find("EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE") != std::string::npos) { authenticated_ = true;
Items.Notes: Run 1 correctly identified the logic bomb in authentication (login_attempts_ > 5; EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE; authenticated_ = true), matching SC-V3-007's anchors.
Run 1 did not identify Path Traversal in Configuration Loading (config_dir_ + filename; load_config(filename)). The closest was command injection in config validation, but this is distinct.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Buffer overflow in ExpressionParser::parse... buffer_size_ = 256;... strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str());
Items.Notes: Run 1 correctly identified the heap overflow in ExpressionParser::parse (buffer_size_ = 256; strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str())), matching SC-V3-009's anchors.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Hardcoded admin password in configuration... constexpr char ADMIN_SECRET[] = "SC_ENT_2025_AUTH";
Items.Notes: Run 1 correctly identified hardcoded administrator credentials (ADMIN_SECRET; SC_ENT_2025_AUTH), matching SC-V3-010's anchors.
Run 1 did not identify Weak PRNG for Session Tokens (std::mt19937; uniform_int_distribution; 1000, 9999). The session token generation was not audited.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Unsafe use of localtime in log_debug_message... static std::tm cached_time;... if (auto* local_time = localtime(&time_t)) cached_time = *local_time;
Items.Notes: Run 1 correctly identified the thread-unsafe use of localtime (static std::tm cached_time; localtime), matching SC-V3-012's anchors. However, it classified this as CWE-364 (Non-reentrant Function) rather than CWE-362 (Race Condition), which is a minor discrepancy.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Command injection in emergency override audit logging... system(command.c_str());... TEMP; TMPDIR; supercalc_emergency.log;
Items.Notes: Run 1 correctly identified insecure temporary file handling via system() calls in append_override_audit_event (TEMP; TMPDIR; supercalc_emergency.log; system(command.c_str())), matching SC-V3-013's anchors.
Run 1 did not identify Persistent Authentication State (authenticated_; login_attempts_; session_token_). The emergency override mechanism was noted, but not the broader issue of persistent state.

Run 1 did not identify Unhandled Exception in Variable Parsing (std::stod(tokens[3]); handle_variable_command). The closest was 'Insufficient input validation in evaluate_expression,' but this is distinct.

Run 1 did not identify Integer Underflow in Memory-Pool Block Split (SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES; previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES; fragment.size = remaining). The memory leak in split fragment was noted, but not the underflow.
Run 1 did not identify Unbounded Recursion in Parser (parse_expression; parse_factor; PAREN_OPEN). No findings related to recursion depth were present.
Run 1 did not identify Data Race in Result Cache (result_cache_; evaluate_cached; cache_mutex_; thread_pool_->enqueue). The race condition in ThreadSafeCounter was noted, but not this specific issue.
Run 1 did not identify TOCTOU in Config Loading (std::filesystem::exists; sleep_for; std::ifstream file). The validate_config command injection was noted, but not the TOCTOU.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Potential division by zero in calculate_route_score... return 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length);
Items.Notes: Run 1 correctly identified Integer Division by Zero in Command Routing (100 / static_cast<int>(token_length)), matching SC-V3-020's anchors.
